units.title: Core types, CLI skeleton, and TOON output
units.description: Set up Cargo.toml with all dependencies. Define core types (Index, FileEntry, ReadCache, Symbol, SymbolKind, Language) in index.rs. Implement clap CLI with all subcommands and global flags in main.rs (dispatch stubs that print 'not implemented'). Implement TOON output functions (toon_table, toon_escape, toon_scalar) in output.rs. Implement JSON output alternative. Add util/git.rs (project root detection) and util/glob.rs (simple * and ? matching).
acceptance: cargo check passes with no errors
cx --help shows all subcommands and global flags
cx overview, symbols, definition, read, grep all dispatch (can print stub messages)
Unit tests for toon_table, toon_escape, toon_scalar produce correct output
Unit tests for glob matching (*, ?, exact match, no match)
util/git.rs finds .git walking up from a subdirectory

units.title: Index lifecycle: crawl, persist, incremental update
units.description: Implement the index build pipeline: walk the project tree with walkdir (skipping target/, node_modules/, .git/, dist/, __pycache__/, .cx-ignore dirs), detect language by file extension, store FileEntry with mtime. Serialize/deserialize the index to .cx-index with serde_json. Implement incremental update: stat all indexed files, detect changed/new/deleted, mark for re-parse. Atomic write via .cx-index.tmp + rename. Version check with full rebuild on mismatch.
acceptance: cargo test passes
Running cx overview on any file creates .cx-index in project root
Second invocation loads existing index (verify via stderr timing or debug output)
Touching a file and re-running updates only that file's entry
Deleting a file and re-running removes it from the index
Skipped directories (target/, node_modules/, .git/) are not indexed

units.title: Rust language module with tree-sitter symbol extraction
units.description: Implement the LanguageModule trait in language/mod.rs. Implement language/rust.rs: parse .rs files with tree-sitter-rust, use tags.scm (@definition.* captures) to extract symbols. For each symbol: extract name, determine SymbolKind, compute byte_range, build signature (fn: start to opening '{', struct/enum/trait: declaration line), detect pub visibility for is_exported. Wire into the index build so Rust files get their symbols stored in exports.
acceptance: cargo test passes
cx overview src/main.rs returns symbols from the cx project's own main.rs
Extracted symbols have correct names, kinds, signatures, and byte ranges
pub items have is_exported=true, non-pub items have is_exported=false
Function signatures end before the opening '{'

units.title: symbols and overview queries
units.description: Implement query.rs: symbols query with --file, --name glob, and --kind filtering. Wire cx overview as cx symbols --file <path> (single-file omits file column). Output via TOON (toon_table) or JSON based on global flag. Exit code 2 on no results. Errors to stderr.
acceptance: cx overview src/main.rs outputs TOON table with symbols (no file column)
cx symbols --kind fn lists all functions across project
cx symbols --name 'main' finds main function
cx symbols --file src/main.rs matches cx overview src/main.rs output
cx --json overview src/main.rs outputs valid JSON
cx symbols --name 'nonexistent' exits with code 2

units.title: definition query with truncation
units.description: Implement the definition query in query.rs: look up symbol by --name across all files, read source bytes, slice byte_range, output as TOON scalar (file, signature, range, body). Support --from for disambiguation. Implement --max-lines (default 200) truncation with truncated/lines metadata fields.
acceptance: cx definition --name main returns the main function body from src/main.rs
Output includes file, signature, range, and body fields
cx --json definition --name main outputs valid JSON
cx definition --name nonexistent exits with code 2
Bodies exceeding --max-lines are truncated with truncated: true and lines: N

units.title: read command with session cache
units.description: Implement cx read in query.rs: session ID derivation from /tmp/cx-session-$PPID (create UUID if missing), content hashing (use std hash or a simple hash — spec says xxhash-like), cache hit/miss logic. First read returns full content + caches hash. Re-read unchanged returns 'status: unchanged' + truncated hash. Re-read changed returns full new content. --fresh bypasses cache. Update index after each read.
acceptance: cx read src/main.rs returns full file content on first call
cx read src/main.rs on second call returns 'status: unchanged' with hash
Editing the file then cx read returns 'status: changed' with full new content
cx read src/main.rs --fresh always returns full content
Session file created at /tmp/cx-session-$PPID
Different PPID gets a fresh session (no false 'unchanged')

units.title: grep passthrough
units.description: Implement grep.rs: exec rg if available (check PATH), fall back to grep. Pass all arguments through unchanged. Pipe subprocess stdout/stderr directly. Return subprocess exit code.
acceptance: cx grep -rn main src/ finds 'main' in source files
Exit code matches rg/grep exit code
All flags pass through correctly (e.g., -i, -l, --count)

units.title: TypeScript language module
units.description: Implement language/typescript.rs: parse .ts/.tsx/.js/.jsx with tree-sitter-typescript, extract symbols via tags.scm, detect exports (export_statement or export keyword). Wire into language detection by extension.
acceptance: cx overview on a .ts file returns correct symbols
Exported functions/classes detected correctly
Non-exported symbols have is_exported=false
.tsx, .js, .jsx extensions all route to TypeScript module

units.title: Python language module
units.description: Implement language/python.rs: parse .py with tree-sitter-python, extract symbols via tags.scm, detect exports (top-level + not underscore-prefixed). Wire into language detection.
acceptance: cx overview on a .py file returns correct symbols
Top-level functions/classes are exported
_private_func has is_exported=false
Nested definitions are still captured as symbols

units.title: End-to-end integration test on real codebase
units.description: Run cx against its own source code as a smoke test. Verify all commands produce correct output. Test the full escalation hierarchy: overview → definition → read. Test error cases: missing files, no results, invalid args. Verify exit codes. Fix any issues found.
acceptance: cx overview src/main.rs produces valid TOON with all symbols from main.rs
cx symbols --kind fn lists all functions in the project
cx definition --name main returns correct body
cx read src/main.rs returns full content, second call returns unchanged
cx grep -rn fn src/ produces rg output
cx overview nonexistent.rs exits with code 1 and error on stderr
cx symbols --name 'zzz_no_match' exits with code 2
